text: When you work with APIs, you use HTTP requests to communicate with a server. The most common types are GET to retrieve data and POST to send data. The server often returns information in JSON format, which is easy for programs to read. Many APIs require authentication, usually through an API key, to control access. You should also be aware of rate limits, which restrict how many requests you can make in a certain time. Finally, always include error handling in your code. If a request fails, a well-designed program can retry it or show a helpful message instead of crashing.
